title: telanthera polygonoides var. radicans
intro: Telanthera polygonoides var. radicans, also known as creeping telanthera, is a type of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Central and South America and is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues, and is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves. In herbal medicine, creeping telanthera is typically prepared by drying the leaves and stems, which can be steeped in hot water to make a medicinal tea.
